description_long: This research institute features a striking geometric design composed of multiple hexagonal towers of varying heights, each topped with a sharp, conical roof. The exterior is dominated by alternating dark and light horizontal bands, giving the structure a layered, futuristic appearance. The towers are clustered closely together, forming a compact and visually complex footprint on a flat, green base that suggests landscaped grounds. The base also features angular, patterned insets that add visual interest and may represent walkways or plazas. The overall form is symmetrical from several angles, with the tallest tower centrally located and flanked by shorter structures. The building's design suggests a focus on modernity and innovation, with no visible windows or doors from these views.
description: A modern research institute with clustered hexagonal towers, layered dark and light bands, and sharp conical roofs, set on a patterned green base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central, tallest hexagonal tower flanked by several shorter towers, with a clear view of the patterned base in front.
description_view_1: This view highlights the dense clustering of the towers and reveals more of the patterned ground area to the right.
description_view_2: The left side view emphasizes the staggered heights of the towers and the angular ground pattern extending outward.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the symmetry of the tower arrangement and the prominence of the central tower, with less patterned ground visible.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is not clearly visible in any view; no doors, steps, or accessibility features are apparent from the exterior.
